none: Raccoon Mountain pumped-storage plant : environmental impact statement.

The Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) proposed the construction and operation of a pumped-storage plant on Raccoon Mountain in Marion County, Tennessee. This project involves converting rural land into an industrial site, impacting 1,000 acres of land and wildlife habitats. The plant will use water from a man-made reservoir on the mountain to generate electricity during peak demand times. While there are environmental concerns, such as wildlife disruption and potential water quality issues, the project is deemed necessary to meet the growing electricity demands in the region.
Raccoon Mountain pumped-storage plant : environmental impact statement.

The Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) proposed the construction and operation of a pumped-storage plant on Raccoon Mountain in Marion County, Tennessee. This project involves converting rural land into an industrial site, impacting 1,000 acres of land and wildlife habitats. The plant will use water from a man-made reservoir on the mountain to generate electricity during peak demand times. While there are environmental concerns, such as wildlife disruption and potential water quality issues, the project is deemed necessary to meet the growing electricity demands in the region.

The Raccoon Mountain pumped-storage plant was proposed by the Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) to address increasing electricity demands in the region. The project faced public pushback due to concerns about its environmental impact, particularly regarding wildlife habitats and water quality. Despite these concerns, the TVA moved forward with the project, emphasizing its necessity for energy generation during peak demand times.

"It is unfortunate that the project will result in a change of land use of approximately 1,000 acres from rural nonfarm use to industrial use, and thereby modifying or eliminating a certain amount of wildlife habitat." — Marvey Bray, Director, Tennessee Wildlife Resources Agency
